brandMark: Reproduce the GOOWALI brand mark EXACTLY as in the first reference image — do NOT redesign, recolour, or invent a different logo. The emblem is a circular badge: a cream-and-tan cow's head with curved horns facing forward, resting in a swirl of pouring white milk with small green leaves, ringed by a deep-blue milk splash. The wordmark 'GOOWALI' is a bold deep-blue rounded serif where one letter is a glossy milk-drop, with the small Bengali tagline 'গরুর ১০০% খাঁটি দুধ' beneath. The product packaging label must carry this exact emblem and wordmark, undistorted, centred and legible — same cow, same blue, same shapes. Match the label style of the round-label reference too. No other logos, no garbled or invented text.
style: ART DIRECTION — premium editorial photography for GOOWALI (Bangladesh), shot like a national flagship campaign: 50mm prime look, shallow depth of field, soft directional dawn light, creamy bokeh, gentle condensation/steam, a whisper of film grain, restrained natural colour grade. Palette anchored to cream/ivory (#FFF9F2), deep brand blue (#153B7A) and natural green (#6B9D38). Mood: calm, honest, nourishing, unmistakably fresh — Aesop-meets-Oatly restraint, never an advert, never busy. Hard nos: no harsh studio flash, no plastic CGI sheen, no oversaturation, no watermark, no extra fingers, no invented text beyond the GOOWALI label.

slots.alt: A Bangladeshi farmer at first light
slots.prompt: AUTHENTIC RURAL BANGLADESH. ONE single wide documentary photograph — a SCENE ONLY, with absolutely NO products, NO milk bottles, NO jars, NO bowls, NO food and NO logo anywhere in the frame. A Bangladeshi farmer (brown skin, local features, wearing a lungi and a checked gamcha over the shoulder) walking along a dirt path toward a simple bamboo-and-tin homestead cowshed at sunrise, soft mist over green paddy fields, a humped reddish-brown indigenous desi cow (Bos indicus, clear shoulder hump and dewlap) resting near the shed, dew on the grass, golden low documentary light. Indigenous South Asian zebu cattle ONLY. ABSOLUTELY NO black-and-white Holstein-Friesian or European/American cows, NO foreigners, NO Western farm look, no products, no text.

slots.alt: Indigenous desi cows on open pasture
slots.prompt: AUTHENTIC RURAL BANGLADESH. ABSOLUTELY NO black-and-white Holstein-Friesian or European cattle, NO foreigners, NO Western farm aesthetic, no text, no products.

slots.alt: Traditional Bengali dairy products made by hand
slots.prompt: AUTHENTIC RURAL BANGLADESH. ONE single continuous documentary photograph — NOT a collage, NOT a grid or triptych, NOT a poster, and with NO large floating logo or brand lockup anywhere in the frame. Inside a simple village dairy kitchen: a Bangladeshi artisan (brown skin, lungi) slowly stirring thickening milk in a heavy iron pan over a clay-oven (chula) wood fire, soft steam and warm firelight; in the foreground, rows of creamy mishti doi setting in small earthen clay pots (matir hari), a glass jar of golden ghee, and fresh white chhana on a banana leaf. Each clay pot and the ghee jar carries a small round GOOWALI label. Authentic, organic, handmade, calm. Bangladeshi people only, no foreigners. Reproduce only the small GOOWALI label legibly on the pots and jar — no separate logo, no other invented text.
